quotes: After first mention of "dermatomyositis," use "DM" in running copy to avoid repetition.
Do NOT use "DM" in headlines, headings, or subheads
every standalone section must establish `dermatomyositis (DM)` at its own first mention

rules.intent: Maintain clarity and accessibility across independently assembled sections.
rules.rule_text: After the first mention of 'dermatomyositis' as 'dermatomyositis (DM)' within a standalone section, use the abbreviation 'DM' in running copy. Never use 'DM' in headlines, headings, or subheads.

quotes: say "someone with dermatomyositis" / "people with dermatomyositis" — not "patients."
say "someone with dermatomyositis" / "people with dermatomyositis" — not "patients."
"Patients" is acceptable only in HCP materials.
Use "care team" (not "staff") for the network of patients, HCPs, and support systems.

rules.intent: Ensure patient-centric, empathetic, and inclusive language.
rules.rule_text: In patient/DTC materials, refer to patients as 'someone with dermatomyositis' or 'people with dermatomyositis' instead of using the word 'patients'. Refer to the medical network as 'care team' rather than 'staff'.

quotes: Exceptions — always numerals: percentages, ages, ratios. Percentages use the % sign (e.g., 74%).

rules.intent: Maintain consistent and clear numerical formatting.
rules.rule_text: Spell out numbers from zero to nine in running copy, but use numerals for 10 and above. Always use numerals for percentages (using the % sign), ages, and ratios.

quotes: when citing clinical data, avoid negative or fear-based language; use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying.

rules.intent: Foster reassurance and alignment with clinical efficacy reporting guidelines.
rules.rule_text: When presenting clinical data, employ an empathetic, supportive, and trust-building tone, avoiding fear-based or overly negative framings.

quotes: Oxford/serial comma always in series of 3+.

rules.rule_text: Always use the Oxford (serial) comma in lists of three or more items.
